texto: Since the 1970s, there has been a continual tendency to over-estimate the surveillance capacities of new technologies. In the sense of the physical invasion of privacy, surveillance comprises five sequential events: the capacity to observe; the act of observation; comprehension of what is seen; intervention on the basis of that knowledge; and a consequent change of behaviour by the subject. Too often the final four have been assumed from the possibility of the first.
explicacao: Citação de David Vincent sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. “Since the 1970s, there has been a continual tendency to over-estimate the surveillance capacities…” articula sabedoria, internet, privacy em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e termino.
